Sword of the Morning  
 

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Skill: 1H Slashing Atk Delay: 27
DMG: 16 Dmg Bonus: 35
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
DEX: +10
Effect: Tishan's Clash (Combat, Casting Time: Instant) at Level 50
WT: 1.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: PAL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)
Sword of the Morning
Lore Item Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Skill: 1H Slashing Atk Delay: 27
DMG: 16 Dmg Bonus: 35
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
DEX: +10
Effect: Tishan's Clash (Combat, Casting Time: Instant) at Level 50
WT: 1.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: PAL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Item Lore:Sword of the Morning (55+)
Item Type:1H Slashing
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:30 pp 0 gp 0 sp 0 cp
Tribute:1667

Source:Live

Expansion: Ruins of Kunark
Average Price: 1,000pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Very Rare
Level to Attain: 45

Post Comment
BARGAIN HUNT!
QuoteReply
# Jul 15 2004 at 5:56 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1594573640660
175 posts
I just started playing on the Rodcet Nife server(been on Bristlebane since '99) and my first day I found this sword in the Bazaar for only 50pp!!!! I nearly wet myself when I saw it;My heart raced as I hunted down the vendor;and I danced for joy(literally jumping out of my chair) when I purchased it!
Thank you,whoever the vendor was(I was so overwhelmed I hit reset button on my comp;it was still on me when I logged back,but vendor was gone).
Just goes to show,you never know what you can find for a bargain.
Gindorn

"Don't let the Grimlings pinch ye! Ye might get Luclin hydrophobia!"

▲ Page top
RE: when it procs
QuoteReply
# Jul 10 2003 at 1:03 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592760253709
3 posts
Sword of the Morning procs at level 50. The weapon procs a 50hp direct damage spell and a 6 second stun. It's very useful when tanking as I commonly like to chain stun mobs. Excellent for keeping the aggro. With STR buffs I have managed to hit for as high as 76hp in damage. It's an awesome addition to the Paladin's 1H slashing ******** I will be keeping this one for a long time. I paid 2500pp on the Rodect Nife server.

Kanastan Tyrblade
52nd Level Paladin
Sacred Order of Aegis "Our Faith is Our Shield"
▲ Page top
RE: when it procs
QuoteReply
# Oct 10 2003 at 7:12 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591782393356
28 posts
At level 41, my Pally (alt) is getting hits up to about 62 (dex 203)with an average in the high 40s. Looking forward to seeing this thing proc... only 50 damage but the stun will be a welcome addition. I bought this sword when my pally was about level 15 for about 1.4k and have been very happy with it.

Edited, Fri Oct 10 08:17:58 2003

QuoteReply

I just purchased one of these yesterday on Ayonae Ro for 1,000pp. I have Haste Belt so I'm kranking out about 20 dmg/sec optimum (not including damage bonus). Very nice sword for 1K. wow seems there are alot of good deals out there on this. I myself just bought it for 1800pp on the tarew marr server. Our average price is between 4-5k for this. The guy was just auctioning it off. I think he upgraded and didnt really feel like hanging on to it. I turned around and auctioned off my centi longsword (magic) for 1600pp..so i was out only 200pp for this great sword! Good luck and safe travels all.

The Proc and the Proc Damage.
QuoteReply
# Oct 08 2002 at 10:11 AM Rating: Default
Nnanji
Scholar
153 posts
I have the sword on my 45 Pally.

I have 2 questions:

1) 2 people have said that Tisha's Clash Procs on this weapon at level 1) 50 and 2) 60. Can anyone confirm the level when this Proc really does go off? I can confirm it doesn't at level 45. I was told 45 was the level for this and it keeps telling me my "will is not sufficient".

2) I have seen the posts that say Tishan's Clash does a 6 sec stun and 50 DD. Can someone else confirm this?

Thanks for you input in advance.

Edited, Tue Oct 8 10:59:53 2002

▲ Page top
Talk about a buy!
QuoteReply
# Aug 04 2002 at 8:23 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592247022121
Scholar
33 posts
I was perusing the Bazaar earlier today, picking up some new gear for my main Shammy, when a guild mate asked me to see if there was a Sword of the Morning for sale up there. Well, lo and behold there was one for sale.

The sword was being sold for 100pp. Can you believe this? Is it possible? Am I making this up?

No, I'm not making it up. I checked out the stats and couldn't figure why a weapon of this caliber was selling so cheaply. So I just went ahead and bought it for my 31 Pally.

For those of you who don't have Luclin yet, I implore you to get it as soon as you can if you'd like to easily find items to make your PCs much more effective.

I also managed to raise my Shammy's wisdom by 12 and her ac by 16 for less than 1k, and that's including other various stats that came with the gear.

Use the new UI and search out whatever is an upgrade for you. You'll be amazed at what you can find. This I promise you!

Prealia Predacious 55 Luminary of Drinal
▲ Page top
RE: Talk about a buy!
QuoteReply
# Nov 06 2002 at 2:41 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
I bought a sebilite scale coat in the bazaar for 15pp. It was not on a trader, a guy was auctioning it. He then went on to sell a set of donal's vambraces for 25pp.

All cheap deals are not typos. In this guy's case, this equipment was stuff that too him was "too junky for his twink". He apparently didn't want to just destroy it, and didn't want to encourage beggars, so he sold it cheap.
▲ Page top
RE: Talk about a buy!
QuoteReply
# Sep 10 2002 at 12:46 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593362190804
10 posts
That was almost certainly a typo by the person selling it. I've seen lots of crazy deals in the Bazaar... 400pp for a Thick Banded Belt, 250pp for Tribal War Boots, to name two. Sometimes it's hard to tell whether someone accidentally entered the wrong price, or is just overly desperate to sell their wares. In the case of the TBB, that was probably desperation, but the TWBs, I'm fairly certain the seller intented to enter 2500pp. But the deal you described takes the cake. :)

On Tunare, the Sword of the Morning still sells in the 3k-4k range... normally. Even though it doesn't proc until level 50, this weapon will easily overtake the damage output of a Wurmslayer by the mid-30s. And it looks pretty sweet too, especially when paired with a Sarnak Battle Shield.
▲ Page top
Wurrmy compared to this?
QuoteReply
# Aug 01 2002 at 4:24 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1639959509881
25 posts
Wurmy stinks to this blade for one 40 delay is slow i dont care of its 25dam 40 delay you will do more damage with this blade.
Ive owned one for a long time first off proc is nice as it is a big time taunter.
60 is max you have done? At upper levels ive hit for upper 80s with it.
And last you dont have to depend on haste for this weapon.

▲ Page top
#Ardnahoy, Posted: Jun 07 2002 at 9:18 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) A guildmate gave me this sword to try out. On paper is sounds nice, but in reality this sword isn't so great. For damage/delay, you can't beat a wurmie. The most I've seen this hit for is in the mid 60's. Usually it hits in the low 30's. Wurmie hits tops in the low 100's, and averages in the 60's.
RE: Interesting
QuoteReply
# Apr 13 2003 at 3:16 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592759423962
22 posts
You must have some crappy strength or something. My 22 paladin has a Sporali Defender's Blade (17/37) and he averages about 38 dmg per hit, max hit for 46. He is slightly twinked with 154 str using yaulp but that is all. Get some more str items before you post some average damage on good weapons and say they stink.

A side note
QuoteReply
# Nov 30 2001 at 4:18 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591765874187
5 posts
.. on a side note, you also get to use a shield.. and there *are* some seriously cool shields out there these days. More AC, stats, and you get to bash. :)
▲ Page top
RE: A side note
QuoteReply
# Dec 31 2001 at 4:08 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249973991
86 posts
yes u do get to use a shield which is why many pallies prefer to use a 1hs over a 2hs, but now high lvl pallies (lvl 59???) get 2h bash which will completely make 1hs weapons insignificant.
▲ Page top
RE: A side note
QuoteReply
# Feb 01 2002 at 5:32 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592253233734
1 post
Even with two hand bash, one hand slashing won't be insignificant. Using 1hs and a shield gives your ac a nice boost. I use 2hs when I'm mostly just trying to dish out as much damage as I can, and 1hs with a shield when I'm tanking.
▲ Page top
RE: A side note
QuoteReply
# Jan 08 2002 at 9:27 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249352913
60 posts
Well, you have to spend quite some time getting AA exp before getting to the 2 Handed Bash ability which is a Level 59 one.

Anyway, I always have an 1hs set and a 2hs set with me, the 2hs more strength = attack + damage oriented, the 1hs more AC oriented.

The 2hs set I use when I am not the tank and
a) am not supposed to get aggro
b) want to deal damage, which is better after 51 with the increased 2hs damage table for Paladins

The 1hs set I use when I am the tank and
a) want to have as much AC as possible
b) want to do as many attacks as possible (because the number of successful attacks influence aggro from my knowledge)

/drops 2cp
▲ Page top
RE: A side note
QuoteReply
# Jan 19 2002 at 8:33 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1671409140606
569 posts
You can always use Flash of Light and all of the stuns paladins get (Stun, Cease, Desist, Holy Might, Force....), and normal taunting to keep aggro. Nothing like magical stuns to get monsters psd off at you. I have noticed how sensitive those Umbral Toilers are to stuns. Many times I have only stunned them once while they are on a warrior, and I get serious aggro. Sheesh calm down little alien! lol
▲ Page top
Uber
QuoteReply
# Nov 30 2001 at 4:13 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765874187
5 posts
There *are* better weapons out there, of course... but that can be said by practically anyone. Very few people will ever get "the best weapon in the game" for their class, whatever that may be... and even then, people will argue over ratio and bonuses and procs to no end.

End of story is that this sword is ungodly. Uber. L33t. Ph4t. Superpimp. Whatever you want to call it. First off, it has a better than 50% ratio, at 16/27... in *ONE HAND*. I've had Warriors and Rangers alike screaming about how they'd give up to four times the price of the Morning for a sword like it they could use. Even though paladins can't dual wield, the fact that this thing is fairly quick helps paladins contribute to taunt (which is the main point of tanks on raids).

Then you see that it has a proc that does a fair amount of damage.

Then you throw in the fact that it's a stun in addition to damage. Stuns are not only extremely useful, but they're also high-degree taunts. Any paladin knows this.

Then you add in the +10 Dex on the sword, which (among other things) improves proc rate.

.. now, are there better swords out there? Uh, yeah. However, there's *ALWAYS* going to be a bigger and better thing to use, particularly with Luclin coming out. Paladins, worship this sword if you get the chance to nab it. This is a taunt machine as well as whipping out great damage. Best of both worlds. :)
▲ Page top
5 min before patch
QuoteReply
# Nov 08 2001 at 10:20 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592251224836
View User Forum
105 posts
Im trying to gather loot in FM with my Guildy Pally friend, for one of these. I dueld a Pally with one and it proc'd like 9 times. its a 6 sec stun and 50dd dam. Pretty nice! BTW the pally kicked my ***** but thats ok. prob more fearsome fighting high HP mob's. High Agro, but thats the point right?

Futhermore peeps in Inny sever is selling them for 8k. I think Pallys should get discounts cause they are so Honnorable class. "as long as they play there char right" Pallys are cool.
2 thumbs up :-D

Applejuce Tolanheart
40 Ranger
Seekers Of Lore
Inny Server

▲ Page top
RE: 5 min before patch
QuoteReply
# Nov 24 2001 at 3:53 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591771543481
Scholar
66 posts
Paly's do get discounts, it's part of their naturally higher charisma. NPC vendors buy/sell to them cheaper.

As for players. Yeah paly's should get discounts... but ow wait.

Clerics should get discounts cause we want to keep the gods happy right? (plus probably some kind of tax write off thing)

OwOw but Ogres should get discounts cause their intimidating, lets face it do YOU muck with someone who is EIGHT TIMES your mass of muscle?

No wait Monks should get discounts because they've got an inate awesome bargoning position (they don't need gear 1/2 as much as other classes).

I could justify to you why any class should deserve a discount. Hence why Verant was wise and made npc vendors repsect charisma, and then gave paly's a charisma bonus.

And we ALL know how much a differance that makes :)

TLE
▲ Page top
I love this sword
QuoteReply
# Nov 07 2001 at 7:00 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592757035640
View User Forum
Scholar
116 posts
I have always wondered if I should buy an SoM or a Frostbringer. Well my decission was made and I went for the SoM. OMFG, I love this sword. I have used it mainly in Velks because I have only had this sword for 4 days, but it produces mad procs. Damn, I have even added divine might and I proc between 4 and 8 times or more per mob in Velks. And with me casting both stun and holy might, not to mention bashing with a shield, as well as Tashan Clash and divine might proc... casters beware
/emote Tenniel grins evily
▲ Page top
OMFG
QuoteReply
# Sep 11 2001 at 9:24 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591755823437
28 posts
HEheh.. I just bough this last night on the trib for 8k!!!! OMFG..

Hehe.. I was in HS with my guild.. And me being the only pally was lucky enough to get a guardians mace.. 14/24 proc guard bard/pally only.. Ne who, to my surprise.. I found out the mace was worth ALOT of cash.. So, I sold the mace for about 11k.. I need'd a better BP, since my EBCP was about 30lvls old.. So, I purchased a Froggy Bp fer 4k + my old used,worn EBCP.. So bout 5k..

Then.. I was running through WC with my alt bard, and saw, "WTS Sword of Morning".. I figured hey, I'll ask'em.. I say "How Much".. HE tells me 8k.. I'm like OMFG.. Ne who.. I paid him 7969pp for it.. It was a chanter, guessing with a twink warrior, cuz she ask'd if I had a lammy to trade..

Ne who.. hehe.. I went and tested it out on Sgt. Slate.. I got about 15 procs in that fight.. That doesn't include the 2 procs that didn't stcik, and doesn't include the Divine Might procs... My Dex is 140.. So, I get boon'd and I'm sure this will proc like mad.. hehe

On a side note.. I think we pallies should rally together and get VI to make Boon and DM stack.. For those of you who don't know why it doesn't stack, it's because Boon has a spell based proc, and DM is a spell based proc.. I would much rather have the DM then a life tap.. hehe

And the other peep who posted there are better weapons out there now it right, how ever if your in a small guild and don't get to raid places like Sleep's,ToV this is prolly the best 1hs you can get..

Ne who, sorry for rambling on.. Just really excited about getting this weapon.. Have fun..

Wolvbane
51 Cavalier of the Tribunal
"Circle of Lemmings"
"almost a hero of the coldain"
▲ Page top
Som
QuoteReply
# Sep 10 2001 at 2:45 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591763505270
8 posts
First of all this sword has a proc of 50DD stun. Which is a good proc. Next yes it procs a LOT. And with boon it procs like mad, BUT you cant have boon and divine mignt, they dont stack. Anyways basically this sword is real good against caster mobs that are below 55. Most mobs above 55 are unstunable so the SOM is bacically worthless. There are many other 1hs out there now that have a better dmg/dly now. No drop darkmetal falchon, dragon-claw shard, sword of blessing, and ToV weapons galore. Of course those weapons are much harder to get except the sword of blessing. A no drop sword in VL.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 13 2001 at 3:09 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Pls send tell to Galeedor in Sol Ro.
